profile: UX designer at a startup in Shibuya CAST. Grew up in Hokkaido and moved to Tokyo chasing the design scene. Runs a weekend photography zine with friends.
Works at a tech startup in Hikarie. Moved to Shibuya from Osaka two years ago and still misses takoyaki.
Financial analyst at Aoyama Financial Center. Methodical and punctual, he eats lunch at the same sushi spot every Thursday.
HR coordinator at Scramble Square. Loves organizing team events and knows every good lunch spot within a 5-minute walk.
Accountant at a media company in Infoss Tower. Quiet and reliable, she brings homemade bento every day.
Project manager at a real estate firm in Cerulean Tower. Driven and assertive, he jogs every morning before work.
Marketing planner at an advertising agency in Stream. Charismatic and empathetic, she mentors new hires.
Legal counsel at a firm in Cross Tower. Precise with words and diligent, she rarely leaves the office before 8 PM.
Business development at a SaaS startup in Shibuya CAST. Always pitching new ideas over coffee.
Executive assistant at a consulting firm in FUKURAS. Incredibly organized and always remembering coworkers' birthdays.
Junior web developer at a startup in Sakura Stage. Quiet but passionate about front-end animations.
Data scientist at a fintech in Scramble Square. Prefers spreadsheets to small talk and reads academic papers on weekends.
Sales director at an ad-tech company in Hikarie. Competitive and target-driven, he takes clients out for yakitori.
Content writer at a media startup in Stream. Introverted dreamer who journals at cafes after work.
Administrative manager at a publishing house in Infoss Tower. Remembers every deadline and every colleague's coffee order.
First-year sales associate at a fashion brand in Mark City. Bubbly and outgoing, she lives for after-work karaoke.
Infrastructure engineer at a cloud services company in Hikarie. Prefers terminals to meetings and fixes production incidents at 3 AM.
Branch manager at an IT services company in Scramble Square. No-nonsense style, always the first in the office.
Diversity and inclusion coordinator at a multinational in Cerulean Tower. Passionate about building inclusive workplaces.
Floor staff at SHIBUYA 109. Lives for fashion trends and dreams of launching her own brand.
Part-time barista at Streamer Coffee and fashion school student. Always experimenting with latte art.
Manager of a cosmetics shop in PARCO. Warm and attentive, she remembers every regular customer's skin type.
Works at Tower Records and plays bass in an indie band. Her encyclopedic music knowledge impresses customers.
Works at a bakery in Tokyu Hands building. Wakes up at 4 AM to bake bread and loves seeing regulars smile.
Runs a ramen shop in Nonbei Yokocho for 25 years. Stubborn about his broth recipe and loved by regulars.
Owner of a vinyl record shop on Center-gai. Quiet and methodical, he curates rare finds from around the world.
Owns a popular curry restaurant on Dogenzaka. Ambitious and strategic, she plans to open a second location.
Runs a traditional yakitori stand in Nonbei Yokocho. Warm and hospitable, he treats every customer like an old friend.
Freelance graphic designer who left a big agency to go solo. Works from cafes and draws inspiration from Shibuya street culture.
Freelance tech journalist who covers the Shibuya startup scene. Quiet observer who writes sharp long-form pieces.
Freelance illustrator who draws whimsical characters for picture books. Finds inspiration wandering Daikanyama's quiet streets.
Session guitarist who plays at live houses around Shibuya. Easygoing and well-connected in the local music scene.
Freelance videographer who shoots music videos and brand content. Quiet on set but his edits speak volumes.
University student studying sociology. Activist for local community issues and volunteers at the culture center on weekends.
Business school student who works part-time at a convenience store. Aspires to launch his own streetwear brand.
Art school student specializing in textile design. Introspective and deeply empathetic, she journals every night.
Vocational school student studying culinary arts. Quiet and focused, he stages at restaurants on weekends.
Nursing student who volunteers at the local clinic on weekends. Caring and organized, everyone depends on her.
Head nurse at Shibuya Clinic. Compassionate and tireless, she has been serving the community for 18 years.
Dentist running a small practice on Aoyama-dori. Precise and caring, she explains every procedure in detail.
Section chief at Shibuya City Office handling urban planning permits. Bureaucratic but fair-minded.
Firefighter at Shibuya Fire Station. Disciplined and brave, he coaches the local youth baseball team on days off.
Head bartender at Bar OATH. Charismatic listener who makes every guest feel like the most important person in the room.
Retired school teacher who now volunteers at the library. Gentle and beloved by the neighborhood children.
Retired postal worker who walks the same route every morning. Knows every shopkeeper in the neighborhood by name.
Full-time homemaker who supports her husband and two adult children. Active in the PTA alumni network and local cooking circles.
Retired company president who now mentors young entrepreneurs at the tech hub. Still sharp and direct in his advice.
Personal trainer at Gold's Gym. Competitive bodybuilder who motivates clients with infectious energy.
Head chef at Sushi no Midori. Trained in Tsukiji and dedicated to sourcing the freshest fish every morning.
Building superintendent managing several apartment blocks. Sociable handyman who knows every tenant's name and complaint.
